title: API Contract / SDK Packaging Decision
decision_rationale: The API contract is functional and auth boundary is sound for Developer Preview. The OpenAPI document is substantially incomplete (at GL-031 state; implementation is at GL-202+). No SDK/package should be published or claimed as official until after GL-203B closes the contract gap. All allowed public claims are bounded and safe. GL-203B is the required next step before any SDK or packaging work.

auth_modes.description: Authorization: Bearer <operator-token> via PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 (600000 iterations) with SHA-256 lookup-hash pre-filter

current_api_contract_summary.tenant_context: Always server-derived. Operator mode: tenant_id from operator record. Admin-token mode: tenant_id bound to 'demo'. Clients cannot supply or override tenant_id.

endpoint_categories.auth: Returns 403 unless ENABLE_DEMO_ENDPOINTS=true; auth still required
Auth required regardless of demo flag

current_api_contract_summary.cors: Exact-match whitelist only. No wildcard. No reflection of arbitrary origins.
current_api_contract_summary.rate_limiting: Separate auth and API rate limiters configurable via env vars

openapi_alignment_assessment.version_lag: OpenAPI is at GL-031; implementation is at GL-202
gaps_to_address_in_gl203b: Implicit tenant context not documented (server-derived from auth, not client-supplied)
workspace_id reserved/nullable not documented in any schema
Cross-tenant denial semantics (404/empty without existence leak) not described
Production-mode startup fail-closed behavior not reflected
Many newer endpoints not in OpenAPI (agent permissions, approval lifecycle, compliance readiness, provenance v2, institutional auditor export, policy requirements)
Demo tamper endpoint guard behavior (403 when flag is off) not documented
Version mismatch: 0.31.0-rc vs actual GL-202+ state
Grant execution tenant isolation (GL-200C) not reflected
openapi_alignment_assessment.severity: Incomplete but not severely misleading about auth requirements or response shapes for documented endpoints
openapi_alignment_assessment.recommendation: Defer comprehensive OpenAPI cleanup to GL-203B. Do not change docs/openapi.yaml in GL-203.

endpoint_auth_boundary_assessment.admin_token_fails_closed: VERIFIED — GL-201 hardening; startup rejects placeholder tokens in production-like mode
endpoint_auth_boundary_assessment.operator_auth_pbkdf2: VERIFIED — 600000 PBKDF2 iterations, SHA-256 lookup hash pre-filter
endpoint_auth_boundary_assessment.tenant_id_server_derived: VERIFIED — extracted from operator record or bound to 'demo' for admin-token; clients cannot supply
endpoint_auth_boundary_assessment.cross_tenant_denial: VERIFIED — returns None/404 for ID lookups, empty list for list endpoints
endpoint_auth_boundary_assessment.demo_action_requires_auth: VERIFIED — auth check runs regardless of demo flag
endpoint_auth_boundary_assessment.assessment: Auth boundary is sound for Developer Preview. No auth bypass gap found.

tenant_workspace_contract_implications.current_state: tenant_id present on all business resource tables; always server-derived; all list/lookup endpoints filter by tenant_id; workspace_id reserved/nullable
tenant_workspace_contract_implications.client_tenant_override: NOT SUPPORTED — tenant_id is never client-supplied
tenant_workspace_contract_implications.cross_tenant_denial_semantics: 404/None for ID lookups; empty list for list endpoints; no existence leak
tenant_workspace_contract_implications.workspace_id_status: Reserved, nullable, not enforced — deferred to future issue
openapi_documentation_needed: Document tenant_id as implicit from auth
tenant_workspace_contract_implications.stale_claims_found: README.md, AGENTS.md, SECURITY.md, llms.txt state 'Tenant isolation is not implemented' — now inaccurate; baseline implemented in GL-200A/B/C
tenant_workspace_contract_implications.stale_claims_action: Document in GL-203; correct in GL-203B or narrow companion task
remaining_gaps: Secondary-path tenant enforcement for evidence/provenance routes (deferred)
Database-level row security (deferred, requires major architectural work)

auth_config_contract_implications.production_mode_fail_closed: VERIFIED — placeholder/weak admin tokens rejected at startup in staging/production mode
auth_config_contract_implications.admin_token_safety: VERIFIED — hmac.compare_digest, never leaked in responses/logs
auth_config_contract_implications.operator_token_safety: VERIFIED — PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256, single issuance, to_dict() exposes only safe fields
auth_config_contract_implications.cors_safety: VERIFIED — exact-match whitelist only, no wildcard
auth_config_contract_implications.secret_leakage: VERIFIED — no raw token values in error messages, startup output, or responses
openapi_additions_needed: Document placeholder/weak token rejection in production-like mode
Confirm no secret values in OpenAPI examples (currently clean)
auth_config_contract_implications.assessment: Auth/config contract is sound. No changes needed in GL-203.

persistence_migration_contract_implicati.sqlite_readiness: READY for controlled SQLite usage within Developer Preview
persistence_migration_contract_implicati.postgres_readiness: Code paths hardened but NOT live-validated; not production-ready for PostgreSQL without live integration testing
persistence_migration_contract_implicati.migration_idempotency: VERIFIED — IF NOT EXISTS guards throughout
persistence_migration_contract_implicati.audit_immutability: VERIFIED — SQLite and PostgreSQL triggers protect audit_events
persistence_migration_contract_implicati.hash_chain: VERIFIED — compatible across pre-migration and post-migration events
persistence_migration_contract_implicati.backup_restore: NOT IMPLEMENTED — no automated rollback; manual DB snapshot required before migrations
api_claims_impact: Live PostgreSQL support cannot be claimed as production-ready without live validation
SQLite mode suitable for Developer Preview

sdk_package_options.label: No SDK/package; examples only
sdk_package_options.when_appropriate: If API contract is still unstable or OpenAPI cleanup not done

sdk_package_options.label: Experimental local SDK prototype later, not published
sdk_package_options.when_appropriate: After GL-203B delivers stable documented API contract

sdk_package_options.label: Public experimental SDK/package later with clear preview caveats
sdk_package_options.when_appropriate: After GL-203B AND GL-203C packaging boundary verification

sdk_package_options.label: Official SDK/package now
cons: API contract undocumented (OpenAPI at GL-031, state at GL-202+)

sdk_package_options.label: Defer SDK until OpenAPI/contract cleanup and packaging tests

selected_sdk_package_recommendation.label: Defer SDK until OpenAPI/contract cleanup (GL-203B), then assess Option B
selected_sdk_package_recommendation.current_sdk_status: sdk/python/grantlayer_client.py is 113-line developer-preview adjacent code; not published; not official
rationale: OpenAPI at GL-031 state; implementation at GL-202+ — contract gap is too large for SDK publication
GL-197 concluded api_first_agent_examples_now_sdk_later — reinforced by GL-203 contract analysis
Existing sdk/python covers health/readiness/grants/audit for Developer Preview adequately
After GL-203B: revisit Option B (experimental local SDK prototype) as GL-203C
Only proceed to Option C (public experimental SDK) after GL-203C verifies packaging boundaries
Only consider Option D (official SDK) after production-ready API contract is verified

agent_integration_implications.agents_md_status: Safe and accurate for Developer Preview caveats; stale tenant isolation claim noted
agent_integration_implications.llms_full_txt_status: Safe, detailed repository map with forbidden areas documented
agent_integration_implications.agent_permission_endpoints: Stateless evaluators, no DB access, no cross-tenant risk
agent_integration_implications.stale_claim: AGENTS.md and llms.txt state 'Tenant isolation is not implemented' — now inaccurate as of GL-200A/B/C baseline
agent_integration_implications.stale_claim_action: Document in GL-203; correct in GL-203B or narrow companion task; no security boundary issue
agent_integration_implications.assessment: Agent integration boundaries are safe as-is for GL-203. No changes to AGENTS.md required in GL-203.

allowed_public_claims: API-first (REST/HTTP, no SDK required to integrate)
Grant lifecycle evidence bundle with cryptographic audit chain
Production auth/secrets/config hardening improved (not production SaaS)
Persistence/migration readiness improved (not production data readiness)
Agent-friendly API surface with permission evaluation and evidence bundles
Audit tamper-evidence with hash-chain verification and immutability triggers
Minimal SDK available for developer evaluation (not published, not official)
prohibited_public_claims: Ready for private grant or institutional data

follow_up_implementation_split.description: Merge gl-203-api-contract-sdk-packaging-decision to internal main after validation
OpenAPI / API Contract Cleanup — update version, document tenant context as implicit, add missing endpoints, correct stale tenant isolation claims in README/AGENTS/llms
SDK Prototype / Packaging Boundary — only after GL-203B; design internal SDK prototype against aligned contract; define packaging boundary tests
Production Ops / Go-No-Go v3 — live PostgreSQL validation, backup/restore minimum drill, observability baseline, go/no-go decision
Deployment / Runtime / Backup / Observability / Admin-Plane — workspace_id enforcement, admin-plane isolation, production-grade indexes, incident response

production_readiness_impact.current_posture: Developer Preview / Controlled Preview with strict boundaries
production_readiness_impact.api_contract: Functional; OpenAPI documentation substantially behind (GL-203B needed)
production_readiness_impact.auth_secrets_config: Hardened for Developer Preview (GL-201); not full production SaaS
production_readiness_impact.persistence_migration: SQLite ready for Developer Preview; PostgreSQL not live-validated
production_readiness_impact.sdk_package: Developer-preview adjacent; not published; not official

risk_register.risk: Stale 'not implemented' tenant isolation claims in README/AGENTS/llms mislead developers about actual baseline
risk_register.mitigation: Document in GL-203; correct in GL-203B or narrow companion task

risk_register.risk: OpenAPI version '0.31.0-rc' vs actual GL-202+ state creates contract confusion
risk_register.mitigation: Document gap; defer correction to GL-203B

risk_register.risk: PostgreSQL not live-validated; deployment risk if used in production without testing
risk_register.mitigation: Document; block PostgreSQL production deployment until GL-204 live validation

risk_register.risk: Premature SDK publication before contract stability creates breaking-change burden
risk_register.mitigation: Defer all SDK publication to after GL-203B; Option E selected

risk_register.risk: workspace_id deferred enforcement creates future migration complexity
risk_register.mitigation: Tracked in GL-200C/GL-203B; column reserved and nullable

risk_register.risk: Secondary-path tenant enforcement for evidence/provenance deferred
risk_register.mitigation: Execution IDs are tenant-scoped at create; direct access requires known ID
Documented; do not promote to production multi-tenant use until GL-200D

findings.summary: OpenAPI version 0.31.0-rc is substantially behind actual implementation state (GL-202+)
Implicit tenant context from auth not documented in OpenAPI
workspace_id reserved/nullable not documented in any OpenAPI schema
Many endpoints added after GL-031 are absent from OpenAPI (agent permissions, approval lifecycle, compliance readiness, provenance v2, institutional auditor export, policy requirements)
README.md, AGENTS.md, SECURITY.md, llms.txt state 'Tenant isolation is not implemented' — now inaccurate; baseline implemented in GL-200A/B/C
sdk/python/grantlayer_client.py is developer-preview adjacent code; not published; not official; adequate for current Developer Preview posture
PostgreSQL code paths substantially hardened but not live-validated; production PostgreSQL deployment blocked until GL-204
Auth boundary verified sound: tenant context server-derived, constant-time comparison, PBKDF2 operator tokens, no secret leakage

recommended_next_issues.description: OpenAPI / API Contract Cleanup — required before any SDK work
SDK Prototype / Packaging Boundary — only after GL-203B
